_source: FAA Pilot's Handbook of Aeronautical Knowledge, FAR/AIM, Aviation Weather Handbook
_note: Long-tail aviation knowledge. Specific regulations, procedures, performance calculations, weather minimums.

class_b_airspace.note: Special VFR available with ATC clearance and 1 mile visibility

class_c_d_e_below_10000.cloud_clearance: 500 feet below, 1000 feet above, 2000 feet horizontal

class_e_above_10000.cloud_clearance: 1000 feet below, 1000 feet above, 1 statute mile horizontal
class_e_above_10000.rationale: Higher speeds at altitude require greater see-and-avoid distance

class_g_day_below_1200.note: Day only; night requires 3 miles and standard cloud clearances

fuel_requirements.ifr: Destination + alternate (if required) + 45 minutes at normal cruise
fuel_requirements.alternate_required: 1-2-3 rule: if within 1 hour before to 1 hour after ETA, forecast is <2000 ft ceiling or <3 miles visibility
fuel_requirements.calculation_example: Flight time 2.5 hours at 10 GPH = 25 gallons + 5 gallons reserve (30 min) = 30 gallons minimum

private_pilot.flight_review: Every 24 calendar months; 1 hour ground, 1 hour flight with CFI

passenger_carrying.day: 3 takeoffs and landings within 90 days in same category/class
passenger_carrying.night: 3 takeoffs and landings to full stop between 1 hour after sunset to 1 hour before sunrise
passenger_carrying.tailwheel: 3 takeoffs and landings to full stop if tailwheel aircraft

instrument_currency.6_month_rule: 6 approaches, holding, intercepting/tracking within preceding 6 months
instrument_currency.grace_period: Additional 6 months to regain with safety pilot (total 12 months before IPC required)
instrument_currency.ipc: Instrument Proficiency Check required if >12 months expired; with CFII

right_of_way_rules.head_on: Both alter course to the right
right_of_way_rules.overtaking: Overtaking aircraft alters course to the right; aircraft being overtaken has right-of-way
right_of_way_rules.landing: Lower aircraft has right-of-way; don't cut in front of aircraft on final

density_altitude.definition: Pressure altitude corrected for non-standard temperature; affects aircraft performance
density_altitude.effects: Higher DA = reduced thrust, lift, propeller efficiency; longer takeoff roll, reduced climb rate, longer landing distance

weight_and_balance.cg_calculation: Total Moment / Total Weight = CG location

example.check: Verify CG within envelope limits from POH

weight_and_balance.fuel_burn_effect: CG shifts as fuel burns; check CG at takeoff, landing, and worst-case fuel state

takeoff_distance.rule_of_thumb: 10% increase in takeoff distance per 1000 ft altitude or 10°F above standard
takeoff_distance.consult_poh: Performance charts account for all factors; always use POH data

climb_performance.vx: Best angle of climb; max altitude gain per horizontal distance; obstacle clearance
climb_performance.vy: Best rate of climb; max altitude gain per time; normal climb after obstacle cleared
climb_performance.speeds_increase_with_altitude: Vx and Vy increase with altitude; meet at absolute ceiling
climb_performance.cruise_climb: Higher airspeed than Vy; better cooling and visibility; lower climb rate acceptable

stable_air.characteristics: Stratiform clouds, smooth air, steady precipitation, poor visibility in haze/fog
stable_air.lapse_rate: Temperature decrease <2°C per 1000 ft (less than standard)
stable_air.flight_conditions: Smooth but poor visibility; ice in clouds; widespread low IFR possible

unstable_air.characteristics: Cumuliform clouds, turbulent air, showery precipitation, good visibility except in rain
unstable_air.lapse_rate: Temperature decrease >2°C per 1000 ft (more than standard)
unstable_air.flight_conditions: Bumpy but good visibility between showers; thunderstorms possible

cold_front.movement: Fast moving (25-30 kts typical); steep slope
cold_front.weather: Narrow band of intense weather; thunderstorms, heavy precip, wind shift, pressure rise, temp drop
cold_front.before_passage: Winds from south/southwest; pressure falling; cumulus development
cold_front.after_passage: Winds shift to west/northwest; pressure rising; temperature drops; clearing
cold_front.flying: Avoid by 100+ miles if severe; fast passage (few hours); good conditions after

warm_front.weather: Wide area of stratiform clouds; steady rain; low ceilings; poor visibility; can last 24+ hours
warm_front.flying: Avoid or plan IFR; widespread low IFR; ice likely in clouds; slow improvement after passage

fronts.stationary_front: Cold/warm front stalled; weather similar to warm front; prolonged poor conditions

lifecycle.cumulus_stage: Updrafts only; building clouds; lasts ~15 minutes
lifecycle.mature_stage: Updrafts and downdrafts; precipitation begins; lightning; hail; most dangerous; lasts 15-30 min
lifecycle.dissipating_stage: Downdrafts dominate; weakening; anvil top spreads

hazards.turbulence: Severe to extreme; can exceed structural limits; avoid by 20 miles
hazards.hail: Up to 5 inch diameter; can occur 20 miles from storm; avoid by altitude or distance
hazards.lightning: Strikes aircraft; brief blindness; electrical damage; EMI
hazards.icing: Severe clear and rime ice; avoid freezing level to -15°C in precip
hazards.wind_shear: Microburst downdrafts 3000+ FPM; loss of 50+ knots airspeed possible; usually <2 mile diameter

thunderstorms.avoidance: Avoid by 20 statute miles; never fly under anvil; never penetrate; use onboard radar/ADS-B

structural_ice_types.rime: Rough, milky white; small droplets freeze instantly; easy to see; breaks off in chunks
structural_ice_types.clear: Smooth, transparent; large droplets spread before freezing; hard to remove; very dangerous
structural_ice_types.mixed: Combination; worst of both; irregular shape; very dangerous

icing.conditions: Visible moisture + temperature 0°C to -20°C; worst at -10°C to -15°C

escape.altitude_change: Descend to above-freezing or climb above clouds if tops known
escape.airspeed: Maintain higher than normal; ice increases weight and drag, decreases lift
escape.flaps: Avoid or minimize; ice can jam or alter effectiveness

icing.fiki_aircraft: Flight Into Known Icing certified; has anti-ice/deice systems; still avoid if able

class_a.requirements: IFR clearance, Mode C transponder, two-way radio
class_a.no_vfr: IFR only; all aircraft IFR separated

class_b.definition: Surface to 10,000 MSL around busiest airports (upside-down wedding cake)
class_b.requirements: ATC clearance before entry, Mode C transponder, two-way radio, private pilot minimum (except student with endorsement)

class_c.definition: Surface to 4000 AGL around towered airports with radar approach; 5 NM inner, 10 NM outer shelf
class_c.requirements: Two-way radio communication established, Mode C transponder
class_c.services: Sequencing to primary airport, traffic advisories, safety alerts

class_d.definition: Surface to 2500 AGL (typical) around towered airports
class_d.requirements: Two-way radio communication established before entry
class_d.no_mode_c: Transponder not required unless within Mode C veil (30 NM of Class B)
class_d.services: Traffic advisories on workload-permitting basis; no separation except runway

class_e.definition: Controlled airspace not A, B, C, or D; typically 1200 AGL (some surface, 700 AGL)
class_e.requirements: None for VFR; IFR clearance for IFR operations
class_e.common: Most airways, extensions from Class D, surface Class E at non-towered fields with instrument approaches

class_g.definition: Uncontrolled airspace; surface to 1200 AGL (or 700 AGL where Class E is 700)
class_g.relaxed_minimums: 1 mile visibility, clear of clouds (day, <1200 AGL); see VFR minimums above

engine_failure.memory_items: Airspeed (best glide), Best field, Checklist, Declare emergency
engine_failure.best_glide_speed: Specific to aircraft (typically 65-75 KIAS for trainers); maximizes glide distance
engine_failure.glide_ratio: Cessna 172: ~9:1; from 5000 AGL = ~8 miles glide distance (no wind)
engine_failure.restart_attempt: Fuel selector, mixture rich, carb heat on, mags both, primer locked, throttle forward; if no restart, secure engine
engine_failure.forced_landing: Aim for large, flat, smooth field; land into wind if possible; avoid wires, buildings, water

procedure: Master switch OFF (cuts power to source)
Fire extinguisher if accessible and fire visible
Land as soon as practicable (not necessarily immediate; assess situation)

vacuum_failure.affected_instruments: Attitude indicator (AI), heading indicator (HI), turn coordinator (if vacuum-driven)
vacuum_failure.flyable_instruments: Airspeed, altimeter, VSI, turn coordinator (electric), magnetic compass
vacuum_failure.ifr: Partial panel procedures; use turn coordinator + airspeed + altimeter; declare emergency; request no-gyro vectors
